clinical note: 82M, PMHx: DM2, HTN, CKD3, CAD s/p PTCA 2015, AF on warfarin, COPD GOLD2, OSA on CPAP, PVD, hypothyroidism on levothyroxine, prior CVA 2018 with residual R hemiparesis. Presented with worsening SOB x5d, productive cough with yellow sputum, fever 38.9C, HR 110, RR 24, O2 sat 88% RA, BP 150/90. CXR: LLL consolidation. WBC 18.2, CRP 145, PCT 2.1, Cr 2.8 (baseline 1.9), BNP 890. Blood cultures x2, sputum MC+S sent. ABG: pH 7.31, pCO2 52, pO2 56. Dx: CAP with type 2 respiratory failure. Started: IV pip-tazo, O2 via Venturi mask 40%, IV furosemide for fluid management, warfarin held, LMWH bridge, TSH ordered.

clinical note: 67F, PMHx: T2DM on insulin, HTN, HL, obesity BMI 38, prior DVT 2020 on apixaban, presented with acute onset R calf pain, swelling, erythema x2d. Doppler US: R popliteal DVT. D-dimer 4200. CBC: Hb 11.2, plt 280. LFT: ALT 45, AST 38. Apixaban dose adjusted per weight, compression stockings, analgesia. Hematology review for recurrent DVT workup.

clinical note: 45M, no PMHx, G6PD deficiency known, presented post-malaria treatment with hemoglobin drop from 12 to 6.5 g/dL, jaundice, dark urine, pallor. Peripheral smear: P. vivax cleared, bite cells+, polychromasia. LDH 1800. Haptoglobin undetectable. USG abdomen: splenomegaly. Dx: G6PD-related hemolytic anemia post primaquine. Transfused 2U PRBC, folic acid started, primaquine discontinued. ICU monitoring.
